title: Easy Baked Corn Recipe With Jiffy That’s Perfect for Family Dinners

description: This baked corn recipe with Jiffy is a simple, comforting side that always wins at family dinners. The secret is the buttery, slightly crispy top that contrasts with the creamy, sweet corn inside. Perfect for holidays or weeknight meals!

pin_variations.title: Why Does This Baked Corn Recipe With Jiffy Taste So Irresistible?
pin_variations.description: Discover the secret behind this creamy baked corn with a golden crust that makes it a family favorite. You'll love how easy it is to make and the buttery flavors that come through. Save this for your next dinner! #bakedcorn #comfortfood #easyrecipes

pin_variations.title: The One Trick That Makes Baked Corn With Jiffy Perfect Every Time
pin_variations.description: Learn the simple trick that keeps this baked corn moist and flavorful, with a crispy, caramelized top. It’s foolproof and perfect for busy cooks wanting a delicious side. Tap to get the full recipe! #easybaking #familydinner #cornrecipe

pin_variations.title: 15-Minute Prep for This Delicious Baked Corn With Jiffy Side Dish
pin_variations.description: Short on time? This baked corn recipe with Jiffy takes just 15 minutes to prep and bakes into a creamy, buttery side everyone will love. Great for weeknights or holiday meals. Save it now! #quickrecipes #comfortfood #bakedcorn

pin_variations.title: Still Making Dry Corn? This Baked Corn With Jiffy Fixes It Instantly
pin_variations.description: Say goodbye to dry corn dishes! This baked corn with Jiffy stays moist and creamy thanks to a buttery mix and perfect baking technique. Your family will ask for seconds. Save this recipe! #comfortfood #easyrecipes #bakedcorn

ingredients: 1 box Jiffy Corn Muffin Mix (8.5 oz)
1 can (15 oz) whole kernel corn, drained
introduction: I first tried this baked corn recipe with Jiffy on a chilly fall evening when I wanted something warm and easy to bring to a family gathering. The mix of whole kernel and cream-style corn with the Jiffy muffin mix creates a wonderfully moist and tender pudding that’s just sweet enough. What makes this version stand out is the addition of melted butter and sour cream, which add richness and a creamy texture that’s hard to beat. Plus, I like to sprinkle a bit of cheddar cheese on top for extra flavor and a golden finish. The trick I learned over time is not to overmix the batter, which keeps the pudding light and fluffy rather than dense. When it’s done baking, you get a beautiful crispy top with a soft, luscious inside that melts in your mouth. It’s perfect for holiday dinners, potlucks, or any time you want an easy side that feels special. My family always asks for seconds, and I love how quick it comes together, especially on busy nights.

instructions.instruction: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a 9x9-inch baking dish with butter or non-stick spray. This step ensures your baked corn won’t stick and helps the edges brown nicely. A well-greased dish also contributes to that irresistible crispy crust around the pudding, so don’t skip it.
instructions.doneness_cue: Dish is evenly coated with a thin layer of butter or spray, no pooling.

instructions.instruction: In a large mixing bowl, combine the Jiffy Corn Muffin Mix, whole kernel corn, cream-style corn, sour cream, melted butter, egg, salt, and pepper. Stir gently just until combined to avoid overmixing, which can make the pudding tough. The mixture should be thick but moist, with visible corn kernels throughout.
instructions.doneness_cue: Batter is smooth with no dry pockets, corn evenly distributed.

instructions.instruction: Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish and smooth the top with a spatula. If using, sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese evenly over the surface for a melty, flavorful topping. This adds a nice savory contrast to the sweet corn and helps form a golden crust.
instructions.doneness_cue: Batter spread evenly, cheese covers surface without clumps.

instructions.instruction: Bake in the preheated oven for 35 to 40 minutes. The pudding should puff slightly and the top will turn a deep golden brown. Avoid opening the oven door too often to prevent temperature drops that can affect rising and texture.
instructions.doneness_cue: Top is golden brown and firm to the touch; edges pulling slightly away from dish.

instructions.instruction: Insert a toothpick into the center to check doneness. It should come out mostly clean with just a few moist crumbs. If it’s too wet, bake for an additional 5 minutes and check again. The pudding will firm up as it cools, so don’t overbake.
instructions.doneness_cue: Toothpick test mostly clean with slight moist crumbs.

instructions.instruction: Remove the baked corn from the oven and let it cool for about 10 minutes before serving. This resting time helps the pudding set and makes it easier to cut into neat squares. The warm, buttery aroma will fill your kitchen and your guests will be eager to dig in.
instructions.doneness_cue: Surface slightly firm but still soft; aroma rich and inviting.

instructions.instruction: Serve the baked corn warm, optionally topped with a pat of butter or a sprinkle of fresh parsley for color. It pairs wonderfully with roast meats, grilled chicken, or as a hearty side for any comfort meal. Leftovers reheat beautifully.
instructions.doneness_cue: Corn pudding holds shape on plate; butter melts on contact.

instructions.instruction: If you want to add a bit of texture, try stirring in 1/2 cup of chopped green chilies or diced jalapeños before baking for a subtle kick. This variation adds a nice depth without overpowering the sweet corn flavor.
instructions.doneness_cue: Chilies evenly mixed; batter consistency unchanged.

instructions.instruction: For a richer pudding, substitute half the sour cream with cream cheese softened to room temperature. Blend it well with the other wet ingredients for a silky, dense texture that’s extra indulgent.
instructions.doneness_cue: Mixture smooth and creamy, no lumps.

why_this_recipe_works.section: Understanding Why This Baked Corn Recipe With Jiffy Succeeds
why_this_recipe_works.content:
This baked corn recipe combines the convenience of Jiffy Corn Muffin Mix with the natural sweetness and moisture of canned corn varieties, creating a pudding that’s both tender and flavorful. The Jiffy mix provides a balanced blend of cornmeal and leavening agents that help the pudding rise slightly while maintaining a soft crumb structure. The cream-style corn contributes a creamy texture and subtle sweetness, while the whole kernel corn adds bursts of juicy crunch.

The addition of melted butter and sour cream is key to the moist, rich consistency. Butter adds fat which tenderizes the crumb and enhances flavor through Maillard browning during baking, producing the golden crust. Sour cream introduces acidity and moisture, which tenderizes proteins and balances the sweetness. The egg acts as a binder, helping the pudding hold together while baking.

Proper mixing is essential; overmixing can develop gluten in the flour components, leading to a tough texture. Gently folding ingredients preserves tenderness. Baking at a moderate 350°F allows the pudding to cook evenly, develop a caramelized top, and set without drying out. The toothpick test ensures it’s perfectly cooked — moist but not wet.

Optional ingredients like cheddar cheese or green chilies introduce savory and spicy notes that complement the sweet corn base, adding complexity. Overall, this recipe’s success lies in balancing moisture, fat, and gentle leavening to create a comforting side that’s simple yet satisfying.

ingredient_notes.Jiffy Corn Muffin Mix: Jiffy Corn Muffin Mix is a classic pantry staple made from cornmeal, flour, sugar, and leavening agents. It’s perfect for this recipe because it provides structure and a subtle sweetness without overpowering the corn flavor. When buying, choose the original mix rather than flavored varieties to maintain balance. The leavening helps the pudding rise slightly, creating a light texture while the cornmeal adds a pleasant grainy feel. Avoid overmixing once combined to keep the crumb tender.
ingredient_notes.Whole Kernel Corn: Whole kernel corn adds texture and natural sweetness to the pudding. Use canned corn drained well to avoid excess moisture that could make the pudding soggy. The kernels provide juicy bursts that contrast nicely with the creamy base. Fresh or frozen corn can be used if drained and patted dry. The corn’s natural sugars caramelize slightly during baking, contributing to the golden crust and rich flavor.
ingredient_notes.Cream-Style Corn: Cream-style corn is essential for the creamy, custard-like texture in this pudding. It contains finely ground corn and liquid that blend smoothly with the batter. Using canned cream-style corn adds moisture and sweetness, making the pudding tender and rich. Avoid substituting with whole corn only, as the texture will be less creamy and more crumbly. The liquid content also helps activate the leavening in the Jiffy mix.
ingredient_notes.Sour Cream: Sour cream adds moisture, fat, and a slight tang that balances the sweetness of the corn. It tenderizes the protein structure, resulting in a soft, moist pudding. Full-fat sour cream is best for richness, but low-fat can be used with slightly less tender results. It also helps emulsify the batter, preventing separation during baking. If you prefer, cream cheese can be substituted for extra richness, but adjust the amount to maintain batter consistency.
ingredient_notes.Butter: Butter contributes fat which is crucial for flavor and texture. Melted butter incorporated into the batter ensures even distribution and creates a moist crumb. During baking, butter promotes Maillard browning on the pudding’s surface, developing a crispy, golden crust that contrasts with the soft interior. Use unsalted butter to control salt levels, and don’t skip this ingredient as it’s key to the indulgent mouthfeel.

pro_tips.tip: Don’t overmix the batter once the wet and dry ingredients are combined.
pro_tips.reason: Overmixing develops gluten in the flour, which can make the pudding dense and tough instead of tender and fluffy. Gently fold ingredients until just combined to preserve a light texture.

pro_tips.tip: Let the baked corn cool for 10 minutes before serving.
pro_tips.reason: Cooling allows the pudding to set properly, making it easier to cut and serve without falling apart. It also lets the flavors meld and the buttery aroma to settle, enhancing the eating experience.

pro_tips.tip: Use a glass or ceramic baking dish for even heat distribution.
pro_tips.reason: These materials heat evenly and help develop a consistent crust without burning. Metal pans may cook too quickly on the edges, risking dryness or uneven baking.

common_mistakes_to_avoid.mistake: Skipping the butter or using too little.
common_mistakes_to_avoid.why_it_happens: Some cooks try to cut fat for health reasons or by accident, thinking it won’t affect texture much.
common_mistakes_to_avoid.fix: Butter is essential for moisture and flavor. Use the full amount melted and mixed in to ensure a tender crumb and golden crust. Omitting it leads to a dry, bland pudding.

common_mistakes_to_avoid.mistake: Overbaking the pudding until it’s dry.
common_mistakes_to_avoid.why_it_happens: It’s tempting to bake longer to ensure it’s done, but the pudding continues to cook after removal.
common_mistakes_to_avoid.fix: Check doneness with a toothpick around the minimum baking time. Remove when mostly clean with moist crumbs, then allow to rest and set.

common_mistakes_to_avoid.mistake: Not draining the canned corn well enough.
common_mistakes_to_avoid.why_it_happens: Rushing prep or not paying attention to moisture content.
common_mistakes_to_avoid.fix: Drain canned corn thoroughly and even pat dry with paper towels if needed to prevent excess liquid, which can make the pudding soggy.

variations.name: Cheesy Jalapeño Baked Corn
variations.description: Add 1/2 cup diced jalapeños and 1 cup shredded sharp cheddar cheese to the batter for a spicy, cheesy twist. This variation adds a nice kick and extra richness that pairs well with grilled meats.
variations.technical_note: Jalapeños add moisture, so reduce sour cream slightly if needed to maintain batter consistency.

variations.name: Bacon and Scallion Baked Corn
variations.description: Mix in 1/2 cup cooked, crumbled bacon and 1/4 cup sliced scallions before baking. The smoky bacon flavor and fresh scallions create a savory contrast to the sweet corn pudding.
variations.technical_note: Ensure bacon is well-drained and scallions finely sliced to distribute flavor evenly without overpowering.

variations.name: Cream Cheese Baked Corn
variations.description: Replace half the sour cream with 4 ounces softened cream cheese for an ultra-rich, silky texture. The pudding will be denser and more decadent, perfect for special occasions.
variations.technical_note: Beat cream cheese smooth before mixing to avoid lumps and ensure even texture.

nutrition.note: Nutritional values are estimates and may vary based on specific ingredients used.

storage_and_reheating.room_temperature: Baked corn can safely sit at room temperature for up to 2 hours if covered loosely to prevent drying out. Avoid leaving it out longer to prevent bacterial growth.
storage_and_reheating.refrigerator: Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days. The pudding may firm up and lose some crispness but reheats well. Serve warm for best texture.
storage_and_reheating.freezer: Freeze baked corn in a freezer-safe container for up to 2 months. Wrap tightly to prevent freezer burn. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.
storage_and_reheating.reheating: Reheat in a 350°F oven for 15-20 minutes until warmed through to preserve texture. Microwave reheating is possible but may soften the crust. Add a pat of butter before reheating to refresh moisture.
storage_and_reheating.make_ahead_strategy: Prepare the batter up to 1 day ahead and keep refrigerated. Pour into the baking dish and bake just before serving to ensure freshness and optimal texture.

faq.question: Can I use fresh corn instead of canned corn?
faq.answer: Yes, fresh corn can be used but it should be cooked briefly and allowed to cool before mixing into the batter. Fresh corn has more moisture, so drain or pat dry to avoid a soggy pudding.

faq.question: What if I don’t have sour cream?
faq.answer: You can substitute plain Greek yogurt or cream cheese for sour cream. Greek yogurt adds tang and moisture but may yield a slightly less rich texture. Cream cheese will make it denser and richer.

faq.question: Can I make this recipe dairy-free?
faq.answer: Yes, use dairy-free butter substitutes and sour cream alternatives like coconut cream or cashew cream. The texture will be slightly different but still delicious.

faq.question: How do I know when the baked corn is done?
faq.answer: The top should be golden brown and the edges slightly pulling away from the dish. A toothpick inserted in the center should come out mostly clean with a few moist crumbs.

faq.question: Can I double this recipe?
faq.answer: Absolutely! Use a larger baking dish and increase baking time slightly, checking doneness with a toothpick. Baking time may extend by 10-15 minutes depending on dish size.

faq.question: What’s the best way to reheat leftovers?
faq.answer: Reheat in a 350°F oven for 15-20 minutes covered loosely with foil to prevent drying out. Adding a small pat of butter before reheating helps restore moisture.

faq.question: Can I add other vegetables to the recipe?
faq.answer: Yes, finely chopped bell peppers or green chilies work well. Avoid watery vegetables like zucchini unless well-drained, as excess moisture affects texture.

faq.question: Is this recipe gluten-free?
faq.answer: The standard Jiffy Corn Muffin Mix contains wheat flour, so it is not gluten-free. Look for gluten-free corn muffin mixes or make your own blend to adapt.

conclusion: I hope you enjoy this easy baked corn recipe with Jiffy as much as my family does. It’s a simple, comforting side that always feels special. Let me know how yours turns out!
